transcript.clean_text: a 160 kilometer transition day leaving tour and ending in chateau who awaited the peloton of the tour de france on stage six mark cavedish in the green jersey awaiting win number 32. a trip through the wall valley 160 case fairly flat all day and finishing in chateau for the first time in a decade the last winner in fact the last two winners in this particular city mark cavendish it was a really frantic start some big name riders got up into an early move as well caspar ascrine threatened the general classification leader of matteo vonner pool for navramat was there and with most of the sprint teams represented the others had to panic and alcare had a chase craig van avermaet got away as the rest were caught and we're all waiting for the next attacks only one came however from roger kluger another really strong rider an olympic track rider these two finding themselves on their own at the front probably had hoped for more company the sprint trains were beginning to form for the intermediate fanarmat and kluger taking the lion's share of the points but 15 still up for grabs there's a little bit of argi bhaji too few riders getting in the way of each other most points taken by colbrelli but several riders coming to line together most keeping their powder dry a few recriminations as well things though would settle down quickly again inside the last 15 kilometers and half a minute the advantage for both kluger and for navramat the penitent not wanting to make the same mistake as two days previous catching the break nervous moments too for jacob guarnieri that man found himself on the ground but thankfully unhurt inside the final two kilometers and it would be the kernick quick step with their world champion julian philippe to take control the final kilometer came and it will be a real battle both left and right of a wide road wildfire not having said he would sprint this time cavemadish still remained favored in the green jersey colbrelli was there ball too as well as phillips in a medley they lead again on lead out duty cavendish coming off the wheel hitting the front of the race guani the closest to challenge him but kavadish winning again the celebration to that first stage when in 2008 almost identical worried about having to retire from the sport eight months previously a fairy tale to see him back at the tour and back to his old best win number 32 now at the tour de france and although he doesn't want us to mention it you have to say now just two stages away from equaling eddie mercs's record the habitual thanks for all the hard work from the teammate then followed and mark cavendish at the top of a tour de france tree yet again phillips the second guani third demar secant fourth and fifth matia von paul still in the yellow jersey two cycling generations both entertainers in their own right thunder pools lead eight seconds ahead of pogacha watford in third 30 seconds and the general classification now moves on in a much more difficult territory stage seven will be the longest tour de france stage for quite some time over 20 years in fact heading to le crossov fairly flat first half of the stage but then the climbs come thick and fast there are bonus seconds as well at a brand new climb at the senior duchamp the tour de france continues to take shape it's been a great race so far now over to you those who need to make moves in the general classification you
